item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, am I looking at the in-canon genesis of the Pataphysics Department?

+1 by the by

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If Murphy even lays a finger on Fred I will hunt him down…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MTF Iota-10 ("Damn Freds")

The first giveaway is perfectly executed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cannot upvote hard enough for ending the article in a terrible, horrible, fantastic pun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So Meta…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is truly mind blowing! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That being said, this wasn't a conflict involving arbitrary, fluctuating power-levels; it was just Murphy realizing he could do what Thaum did to him. I understand finding that framework tedious, but I think it's a bit of a misread to say Thaum has "superior meta-pata powers", then "doesn't"; Thaum's abilities never change in the story. Murphy just realizes that Thaum is as vulnerable to deconstruction as he is.

Yep, that was definitely a misread on my end. In my defense the audio logs looked like no-rules combat roleplay of the kindergarten kind ("my thing is better and beats your thing" - "nuh uh my thing has this cooler thing") because there wasn't quite enough character development between the shift in power-levels except an audio log where Pataphysics Man disses Murphy, but after a reread it's clear that this is not the case.

Either way, rescinded my downvote since that was my main gripe with the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, part of the challenge of this piece was finding a way to do it that would work for someone who hadn't read the previous piece and for someone who did. That saddled me with some weird limitations — limitations that operated to the detriment of the work. I went in knowing it couldn't be quite as strong as the first piece (this is partly why it's so short!).

That being said, this wasn't a conflict involving arbitrary, fluctuating power-levels; it was just Murphy realizing he could do what Thaum did to him. I understand finding that framework tedious, but I think it's a bit of a misread to say Thaum has "superior meta-pata powers", then "doesn't"; Thaum's abilities never change in the story. Murphy just realizes that Thaum is as vulnerable to deconstruction as he is.

Regardless, I appreciate the feedback! I'm very comfortable with this piece as it is, but understanding how someone interprets it (positively or negatively) is still useful. Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1. Novote.

This feels like a "deconstruction" of its' superior predecessor done for the sake of being "meta" or "pata" or what-have-you that doesn't actually have an entertaining story of its' own ("a newly introduced character possesses superior meta-pata powers to a previously established character, then he doesn't. Fin.") I'll put it this way: if 3043 was a perfectly decent movie, this would be an unfunny and nitpick-filled CinemaSins "critique" that somehow managed to get a theatrical run.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The original draft of SCP-3043 was a script (It Always Rains) that was unfinished; the article concerned the Foundation containing Murphy Law by becoming characters in his story and giving him a good ending to go out on (at the end, he died in a tragic-but-awesome way). So yeah, I think you're on point to imagine this is a way to contain him: Give him a story where being contained (or neutralized) makes for a good ending.

Also, yeah, my idea behind Murphy has always been that he's absurdly powerful, able to overwhelm basically any effect — but he's limited by the conventions of his story. Part of the question this narrative was/is heading toward is whether or not his narrative is strong enough to beat SCP-2747.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get the sense that Murphy Law is a very powerful reality warper (for a given value of "reality") who happens to be constrained by the conventions of his chosen genre. He's enough stronger than SCP-3043 that he could have just steamrolled over everything as an unstoppable storm of bullets and hardboiled monologues. But that wouldn't have been noir, so instead he nearly becomes 3043's fall guy and only figures out what's going on at the very end.

And in this article, perhaps Murphy could have overpowered Dr. Thaum's deconstruction and escaped in the very first interview log. But that wouldn't have made for a good story, so instead he gets contained and doesn't break out until the third interview log.

The narrative will continue until SCP-3143's actions lead to a resolution consistent with the genre; at this point, the narrative will conclude and reality will revert.

I wonder if the Foundation could exploit this to contain or neutralize Murphy for good: create a narrative where Murphy's death or incarceration is the only thematically appropriate ending. But I'm not sure offhand if there are any proper noir films (rather than parodies or subversions) where the main character is the hard-boiled detective, and he dies or gets arrested at the end. From what I've seen and read, it's usually it's the supporting characters who die in hardboiled stories, and I don't think Murphy would be anyone's supporting cast.

And even if that does work… would that stop Murphy from using prequels to write himself back into the Foundation's narrative?

item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded: TheeSherm released this wonderful reading on youtube for SCP-3143!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god. This is good to the point of unfair. Like… stop being so good Hippo. Just stop.

Damn freds… too damn clever.

+1. I wish I could +2.

item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded: SunnyClockwork made this fantastic piece of fanart for SCP-3143!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, but he told it what made it tick, so what made it tick changed. By telling him how they captured him, they didn't really capture him at all. Instead, they made it impossible to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Deconstruction, real deconstruction as the term is understood by Derrida and literary analysis in general, doesn't exist to critique and point out shortcomings in one's work, but ultimately makes it stronger by understanding what makes it "tick".

Isn't that precisely what ended up happening, though?

Maybe the whole article was Dr. Thaum performing a proper deconstruction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like our fictional Dr Thaum got caught in a trap. Deconstruction, real deconstruction as the term is understood by Derrida and literary analysis in general, doesn't exist to critique and point out shortcomings in one's work, but ultimately makes it stronger by understanding what makes it "tick".

No surprise that the tables were eventually turned on him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But you're just a horror story dressed up in a lab-coat, glasses, and a funny little accent.

You had me saying "FUCK YESSSS" at this part. Is it even possible to not stop, upvote the fuck out of the buttom and keep reading as soon as possible? No, that isn't even an option.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MTF Iota-10 ("Damn Freds")

Oh shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hippo, you've quickly become one of my new favorite authors. I keep looking up fun new SCP entries, and they're almost all written by you!

I love the half-boiled silliness of Murphy Law and look forward to reading more of his adventures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!

I actually am familiar with Thursday Next, yes! I'm actually a little 'eh' on the book for a bunch of reasons I probably shouldn't elaborate on here, but I'd be lying if I claimed that the 'rules' of Pataphysics and how they interact with Murphy wasn't at least a little bit inspired by it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gosh dangit, Hippo, stop writing such good stuff, you're making some of us feel so inadequate.

This one made me laugh out loud as each new twist came out. I've always been a big fan of metafictional concepts, but this is like, metametafiction. Cripes.

Out of curiosity, have you ever read Thursday Next? I'd be genuinely surprised if there's not a bit of influence from that in this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome!

(Any accent will do, but in my head, I always imagined him as having a (fairly slight) German one.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is some solid usage of the Pataphysics Department with a fitting amount of narrative mindscrew. It's all I hoped would come out of pataphysics and Murphy Law. (also that was some stellar cheesy noir).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The tale goes ever on and on
Far from the plot where it began
Now far ahead the tale has gone
And I must follow if I can…"

This site has some of the most lucid and enjoyable treatments of story as story I've read lately. It's like
sophisticated literary fiction that's also fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the freaking what?

That turn in the final interview was brilliant. This invokes pataphysics in a way that's incredibly satisfying in that it keeps the meta-wankery at arms length.

It is much easier to digest than the previous SCP involving Pataphysics, and I appreciate how accessible it makes the concept to a wider audience who don't have to keep track of half-a-dozen Wiki-specific trends, authors, concepts, and threads. Kudos.

And I totally missed the 423 reference…I have just died.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incredible! I loved SCP-3043, and this was almost as good! The plot twist was excellent, and the little references to Fred at the start and end were clever. Can't wait for part 3!

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
God damn it. You know I loved it before I clicked it, and I wasn't disappointed in the least. The meta/pata twisting, oh man.

Just you wait Hippo, Murphy Law Audio Adaptation round 2 is coming. Just, um… what accent should I give Dr. Thaum? My initial guess was German, but… thoughts?

Edit: It took a while to wrangle my voice actor, but here it is in all its glory!

https://youtu.be/MvAishDsjNI

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes yes yes yes yesssss.

All the +1s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Isn't that just like the Foundation? Brings a metaphor to a pataphor fight."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was meta on like, three different levels at least. @_@ It's pretty nuts.

Fred, what are you doing? D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really am a sucker for this pseudo-noir, and the zooming in on the 'EXIT LOG' section was inspired.

item.title: Re: Author post
item.content:encoded:
Also, just in case anyone wants my thoughts (I'm hoping it's abundantly clear what happened, though):

Author Head-Canon
– hide block

The Foundation tried to contain Murphy Law by creating a fictitious 'Pataphysical Department' with 'Thaddeus Thaum', a skilled literary deconstructionist who would deconstruct Murphy's narrative and give him a banal, containable backstory.

It almost worked.

Murphy was able to deconstruct the deconstruction. He started deconstructing the very concept of the article itself — at which point it all fell apart.

There's also a hidden credits scroll collapsible below the final email. I might make it unhidden later; it's not terribly important, but it kind of hints at how Fred managed to escape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More Murphy is always a treat, great work Hip!

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
For your consideration.

SOURCES:

  • Image from here.
  • Rimple's OverMeta, which is where I stole the whole idea of a fictitious Pataphysics Department (and, let's be honest, is also where I stole the idea of Pataphysics itself).

FEEDBACK:

  • OthellotheCat
  • Sadse
  • TheIncalculableZ
  • ChaoSera (Broken Faith)

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!
